read_policy: Use this file for active backlog, next actions, scanner counts, stable debt ids, and documentation hygiene follow-up. Do not reread archived markdown history unless a debt id points there.

notes: The widget timing matches are the intentional centralized pumpFeatureUi helper plus two calendar test pumps queued for a later testability pass.
Feature tappable scanner now skips built-in labeled buttons, tooltipped controls, and tappables already wrapped in Semantics or Tooltip.
Fixed-white pill CTA scanner reports zero candidates after moving white CTA fills to CatchButtonVariant.light.
Raw range slider scanner reports zero candidates after moving age/pace sliders to CatchRangeSlider.
Raw number stepper scanner reports zero candidates after moving Create Run duration and Edit Profile height to CatchNumberStepper.
Raw Material/Cupertino button scanner reports zero candidates after moving text-only actions to CatchTextButton.
Raw text input scanner reports zero candidates after moving OTP entry to CatchOtpCodeField.
Profile bottom-sheet editor scanner reports zero candidates after moving normal Edit Profile field edits to inline expansion.
Profile inline chip-label scanner reports zero candidates after hiding repeated ChipField labels inside expanded profile tiles.
Profile inline chip Clear-action scanner reports zero candidates after moving nullable single-choice chip clearing to tap-selected-chip-again plus Done.
Profile stacked text-tile editor scanner reports zero candidates after moving normal text Profile rows to in-row value editing.
Profile stacked chip-tile editor scanner reports zero candidates after moving selected Profile chips into the row value slot and showing only available alternatives below.
Profile inline drawers now use ProfileInlineDisclosure/ProfileInlineAnimatedBody for shared open/close motion and dynamic body-height changes.

Profile inline drawers now preserve full width while animating vertical reveal/fade, preventing Cancel/Done action rows from sliding sideways. Shared inline editor panel owns profile editor padding, save errors, and action placement.
Profile row-owned text editors now use ProfileInlineEditableText so active text keeps the closed row style and icon-relative position with only cursor and text-width underline feedback.
The decorated-surface scanner includes the ProfileInlineEditableText underline AnimatedContainer; that is intentional primitive-local focus chrome, not a feature-local card/surface.
The decorated-surface scanner includes primitive-local avatar obscuring/hero texture chrome; those are intentional low-level visual effects, not feature-local replacement surfaces.
ChipField now enforces optionality for empty selections: optional fields may clear, required single-/multi-choice fields keep at least one selected value.
CatchChip now constrains long labels so inline chip editors do not need field-local overflow patches.
The 2026-05-08 scanner snapshot was taken in a dirty worktree with unrelated run-club draft UI changes; broad decorated-surface and unstyled-Text counts include that active work.
Review surface refactor kept raw button/input scans clean and reduced literal SizedBox spacing candidates to 178.
Literal SizedBox spacing, decorated surface, and unstyled Text scans are intentionally broad triage queues for focused feature batches, not automatic lint failures.
Legacy spacing scanner is split between canonical 4-point migration candidates and intentional fine-grained compatibility helpers.
Canonical spacing migration should be piecemeal and touch-driven.
Raw error-surface candidates are clean after the hard CatchErrorText removal and direct primitive migration.
Stream lifecycle watch audit on 2026-05-06 found only one-shot auth/payment protocol timeout usage, not Firestore snapshot listener timeouts.
Error UI watch audit on 2026-05-06 found no CatchErrorText regression and zero scanner raw error-surface candidates.

pending.title: Inventory primary actions by cardinality and scope
pending.summary: Define every primary user action as not allowed, singleton, unbounded, or domain-bounded. Initial policy and audit are in docs/action_cardinality_policy.md. Next pass should turn that table into a maintained action inventory, then audit rapid duplicate taps and unbounded collection display behavior.

pending.title: Introduce typed Dart DTOs for callable Function boundaries
pending.summary: Replace high-friction ad hoc callable maps and response casts with feature-local Dart DTOs. Runs, payments, and Places now have Dart DTOs; keep backend Zod schemas as the server-side source of validation truth, avoid broad generated model churn, and continue reviewing run club/profile callable boundaries before closing this item.

pending.title: Fix profile tab and preview tab blank rendering
pending.summary: The null-profile blank branch now renders an explicit missing-profile state, profile canonical spacing is migrated, and ProfileScreen currently uses NestedScrollView with native TabBarView paging for the Edit/Preview tabs. Normal Edit Profile field edits now expand inline inside the profile list instead of opening bottom sheets; the old profile field bottom-sheet file was removed, nullable single-choice editors keep no chip selected for null values, and regression tests cover inline save/error/pending states. Normal text Profile rows now edit in the row value slot instead of stacking a separate text field below the row, using ProfileInlineEditableText so active values preserve closed-row typography and position with cursor plus text-width underline feedback rather than a boxed field. Profile inline chip editors no longer repeat their row label, nullable single-choice chips can be cleared by tapping the selected chip again, single-choice chip changes now wait for the shared Cancel/Done footer before saving, and selected enum chips now render in the row value slot while expanded so the option list only shows available alternatives. Inline profile drawers now open/close through the shared animated disclosure shell, including dynamic body-height changes for chip edits. The drawer body now preserves full width while animating vertical reveal/fade so action rows do not slide sideways, and the shared inline editor panel owns padding, save errors, and trailing Cancel/Done placement. The Profile header now exposes only Settings; review history, payment history, and sign out live in Settings account rows. The Profile NestedScrollView overlap contract is explicit in code, but physical testing still shows an unresolved bug: when the user scrolls upward from the Preview tab at the top of the internal profile card, the outer Profile header does not reliably return unless the gesture starts near the tab row. Do not mark this complete until the preview-card leading overscroll expands the outer header continuously on device.

pending.title: Align onboarding and edit-profile field coverage
pending.summary: Shared profile validation now covers name, required display name, date of birth, phone, optional email, optional Instagram, height bounds, and open-ended age display/storage. Onboarding initializes `displayName` from first name and explains that first name starts the public display name while last name stays private. Edit profile exposes `Display name` as the first editable About field and keeps identity fields (date of birth and gender) readonly. Public profile rendering prefers `displayName`, then first name, then legacy full-name first token, so last name is not shown publicly. Profile inline editors now save before collapsing and show mutation loading/error feedback. All nullable single-choice edit fields have no-selected-chip coverage when empty; domain decode preserves omitted optional enum fields as null; `ChipField` has a direct empty single-select rendering test; immediate-save single-choice editors show a visible saving state; and failed single-choice saves clear optimistic pending selection before showing inline error. Remaining field coverage is intentionally asymmetric: onboarding captures only low-friction signup fields (name, date of birth, phone, gender, interested-in genders, Instagram, photos, pace range, preferred distances, and running reasons), while edit profile additionally exposes email, height, bio, city, age range, occupation, company, education, religion, languages, relationship goal, drinking, smoking, workout, diet, and children. Do not expand onboarding with those optional fields unless product explicitly accepts the added signup friction.

pending.title: Polish shared public-facing profile card
pending.summary: Implementation polish pass completed on 2026-05-06. Swipes, Profile Preview, and Public Profile still share one `ProfileCard` rendering path. The card remains dark and immersive in both light and dark app themes through `ProfileCardPalette`, shows display name + age + optional city on the hero, moves relationship goal into detail chips, promotes the bio prompt ahead of running stats, and uses a branded missing-photo fallback. `_RunningIdentityCard` is now the single canonical `RUN PROFILE` section; the redundant lower `ProfileRunningSection` was removed. Non-hero photos are inset and rounded inside the card while the hero remains full-bleed. Remaining work is physical-device visual review before deciding whether to continue more opinionated refinements.

pending.title: Add optional sound to full-screen celebration moments
pending.summary: Full-screen celebration moments now share `CatchCelebrationScreen` and `CelebrationEffectsController`, with haptics enabled by default for run creation, run signup/payment confirmation, participant self-check-in, and matches. Sound is intentionally deferred. When product is ready, add sound through `CelebrationEffectsController` only, choose app-owned audio assets/package, respect mute/silent/accessibility expectations, and keep widget tests deterministic through an injected fake effects controller.

pending.title: Apply callable rate limits consistently
pending.summary: Closed on 2026-05-06. `verifyRazorpayPayment`, `cancelRunSignUp`, `joinRunWaitlist`, `blockUser`, `unblockUser`, and `requestAccountDeletion` now enforce shared `checkRateLimit` before expensive or destructive work. `updateUserProfile` is now configured and enforced at 60/minute so field-by-field profile edits remain ergonomic while write loops are bounded. Watch future callables for the same App Check + auth + Zod validation + rate-limit ordering.

pending.title: Complete backend-owned notification fan-out
pending.summary: Core producer work is complete and this queue is now watch-only. Durable in-app activity lives in backend-owned `notifications/{uid}/items/{notificationId}` docs. Match, message, new-club-run, run signup, waitlist-promotion, upcoming run reminder, run schedule/location update, and run cancellation producers are implemented through Functions/callables. Activity reads `watchActivityNotificationsProvider(uid)`, users can only read own timeline plus mark items read, and push delivery is gated by granular user preferences. Club updates use a two-tier model: active membership receives Activity updates, while per-club `runClubMemberships/{clubId_uid}.pushNotificationsEnabled` plus `prefsClubUpdates` gates FCM pushes. Remaining notification work is product/UI policy backfill owned by `RUN-CANCELLATION-UI-POLICY-001`, settings/permission UX refinement, and device QA, not a missing core fan-out gap.

pending.title: Define run cancellation policy and host UI
pending.summary: `cancelRun` now exists as a host-only backend callable: it writes `runs/{runId}.status/cancelledAt/cancellationReason` and sends durable/push cancellation notifications to signed-up and waitlisted participants. Do not expose it in the app until product defines cancellation windows, refund behavior, participant copy, host confirmation/error UI, and how cancelled runs appear on list/detail/calendar/activity surfaces.

pending.title: Retire relationship arrays in favor of edge documents
pending.summary: Relationship/action documents are now the source of truth for run-club memberships, run participations, saved runs, and match-scoped messages. The retired relationship arrays have been removed from Dart models, generated Functions types, Cloud Functions writes, Firestore rules/indexes, active validation/migration tooling, and active tests. Canonical parent docs retain only aggregate projections such as `memberCount`, `bookedCount`, `waitlistedCount`, `checkedInCount`, and `genderCounts`. Keep this item on watch for regressions: do not re-add relationship arrays to models, Functions, rules, active tooling, or tests.

pending.title: Simplify Firestore rules after relationship-doc migration
pending.summary: Rules were simplified alongside relationship-array retirement: direct run and run-club writes are denied, saved-run/profile projections are gone, swipe eligibility uses `runParticipations`, review create/update/delete moved behind callables, and retired array fields are no longer valid schema. Remaining simplification work is narrower: confirm all public surfaces use `publicProfiles`, keep `users/{uid}` owner-readable only, decide whether active `runClubMemberships` should stay publicly readable for member-list product needs, and consider whether chat message create/reset unread should remain the final direct product writes.

pending.title: Add seeded tests and count validation for relationship migration apply
pending.summary: `tool/firestore_relationship_migration.mjs` supports dry-run and apply modes for creating relationship docs and copying legacy chat messages. Before running apply on shared beta data, add seeded fixture tests for duplicates, missing docs, deleted users, legacy chats, and count mismatches, and make apply output validate aggregate counts instead of only reporting planned writes.

pending.title: Rewrite deletion and anonymization around relationship-doc queries
pending.summary: Relationship docs make deletes query-driven. Rewrite account deletion to query runClubMemberships by uid, runParticipations by uid, savedRuns by uid, reviews/payments by user, matches by participant, swipes by source/target, public profile, private profile, Storage, and Auth. Add run deletion and club deletion plans that use runParticipations/runClubMemberships instead of scanning parent arrays.

pending.title: Audit retained tab and route streams for lifecycle ownership
pending.summary: Known Firestore listener lifecycle issues are resolved and this queue is now watch-only. Dashboard booked-runs and run-club streams no longer use idle Stream.timeout, and no remaining lib `.timeout` usage targets Firestore snapshot listeners. Home pauses booked-runs while inactive, AppShell no longer prewarms the Clubs list stream, Clubs/Catches/Chats/Profile tab roots gate screen-owned providers while inactive, run-club stream families are autoDispose, and route-owned providers for run detail, chat messages, match detail, payment history, and blocked users have auto-dispose tests. Keep shell-level streams only when they drive shell-wide UI such as auth, current user, connectivity, FCM, or unread badges. Future realtime streams must be auto-dispose, retained-tab gated, or explicitly documented as global/prewarmed with read-cost rationale.

pending.title: Create and migrate to branded app error surfaces
pending.summary: The canonical branded app-facing error primitive family now exists: `CatchErrorState`, `CatchErrorScaffold`, `CatchSliverErrorState`, `CatchInlineErrorState`, `showCatchErrorSnackBar`, `ErrorBanner.fromError`, and `appErrorDescriptor` / `appErrorMessage` / `appErrorTitle`. The 2026-05-12 backend error hard migration added `BackendErrorContext`, `withBackendErrorContext`, `withBackendErrorStream`, `normalizeBackendError`, `backendErrorMessage`, `backend_operation_failed` analytics, and `tool/backend_error_candidates.dart`; deleted the Firestore/Auth/run-booking-specific message wrappers; and migrated Auth, Firestore, Functions, Storage, FCM, local cache, location, external share/link, payments, and repository/controller boundaries. The completion pass classified the old flat scanner `review=153` queue as `review=0`, `verified=80`, `intentional=29`, `fixture=56`, and `migrated=148`. `docs/error-handling-audit.md` is now the active source of truth. Keep `dart tool/backend_error_candidates.dart --json` at `mustMigrate=0` and `review=0`.

pending.title: Finish app-wide error management beyond backend wrappers
pending.summary: Create the app-wide error-management layer described in docs/error-handling-audit.md version 2.6.0. The next pass should design a non-backend operation-context API, add a scanner for frontend/local/provider/plugin/global error candidates, classify scanner output into mustMigrate/review/verified/intentional/fixture/migrated buckets, then migrate validation, local persistence, route/deep-link parsing, platform/plugin side effects, provider/mutation UI, uncaught async callbacks, and framework/global reporting until the scanner is clean. Do not start ad hoc candidate fixes without making the scanner and this backlog item the source of truth.

pending.title: Close remaining match and chat audit follow-ups
pending.summary: The match preview contract is now backend-owned and the chat list uses one thread preview per match, but remaining follow-ups still need a focused pass: inspect and clean seeded duplicate match/message data for the active phone account, run device QA on the Chats list and thread, decide whether to replace the current matches-count badge with a real presence model or rename it, polish chat tile width/separators/surfaces against shared spacing primitives, keep the new-match horizontal rail queued for product implementation, and audit chat image/media moderation plus profile thumbnail backfill in real data. Do not treat synthetic duplicate data as a UI bug until Firestore documents and seed scripts are inspected.

pending.title: Enforce non-overlapping runs for clubs and users
pending.summary: Implemented on 2026-05-09. Run creation and host schedule edits now enforce non-overlapping active runs per run club. User sign-up and waitlist joins now enforce non-overlapping signed-up/waitlisted user schedules. The overlap model is half-open, so 2:00-3:00 and 3:00-4:00 are allowed. Existing data without schedule locks is still protected by transactional run/participation queries; deterministic per-minute schedule lock docs protect concurrent writes going forward. Host time edits are rejected once signed-up, waitlisted, or attended participation edges exist so user schedule locks cannot be mass-rescheduled into inconsistent state. Deploy Functions before relying on this in production, and decide later whether to run a lock backfill for observability even though the query fallback protects existing data.

pending.title: Expand shared client/server business constants
pending.summary: Initial generated constants now cover run duration, schedule-lock slot size, self-check-in windows, self-check-in distance, and host attendance windows. Continue consolidating duplicated literals as touched: age bounds, profile text limits, price/capacity/distance limits, report text limits, rate-limit policy exposure, media upload limits, and swipe/match eligibility windows. Do not move purely visual constants into this file; this is for product/business invariants that cross the Flutter and Functions boundary.

pending.title: Keep docs index and registry synchronized
pending.summary: Whenever a durable doc owner is added, renamed, archived, deleted, or assigned a new recurring rule, update docs/README.md, doc_versions.json, doc_summaries.json, rules.json, and any affected owner doc in the same pass.

pending.title: Unify top-level tab header and first-content spacing
pending.summary: Screenshots on 2026-05-12 showed repeated spacing drift between title/subtitle, pinned search/tab controls, and first content on Chats, Clubs, Home, Catches, and Profile. Keep long-title composability through explicit CatchSliverHeader height contracts, but centralize the shared title padding, subtitle-to-control gap, pinned search/filter row padding, tabbar-to-content gap, and control-to-first-card gap instead of tuning each screen separately. Fold the 2026-05-12 scanner follow-ups into this queue when in scope: raw TextField candidate in location picker and tappable/semantics candidates in photo slot/location picker.

completed.title: Generate and consume tiny profile thumbnails for run hype avatars
completed.summary: Added backend profile-photo thumbnail generation with a dry-run/apply backfill script, shared `PersonAvatarStack`/obscured avatar support, `RunHypeAvatarStack` provider-owned participant selection from `runParticipations` plus `publicProfiles`, blurred hype avatars in Dashboard next-run and Run detail, and thumbnail-first normal avatars in Chats and match celebration surfaces. Chat image uploads now use the shared `ImageUploadRepository` policy instead of duplicating picker/storage logic.

completed.title: Unify high-emotion success moments under full-screen celebrations
completed.summary: Added `CatchCelebrationScreen` and `CelebrationEffectsController`, migrated host run creation, free and paid user signup confirmation, participant self-check-in, and match creation to full-screen celebration surfaces, and kept host attendance out of the celebration flow. Added an injectable `RunCheckInLocationService` seam so self-check-in remains testable without platform location plugins.

completed.title: Split Home into Dashboard and Activity tabs
completed.summary: Home now follows the Profile-style tab architecture. `DashboardScreen` owns the route-local `TabController`, `NestedScrollView`, collapsible Home header, pinned `Dashboard`/`Activity` tab row, and native `TabBarView` paging. Existing dashboard widgets were extracted into dashboard sliver bodies, Activity moved out of Dashboard content, and the Activity tab renders a timeline-style feed with branded inline retry errors.

completed.title: Audit remaining feature tappable candidates
completed.summary: Reviewed the 24 syntactic tappable candidates. Existing controls already had user-visible text, tooltips, or Semantics wrappers, so the useful fix was scanner refinement. The scanner now reports zero unresolved tappable candidates.

completed.title: Piecemeal legacy spacing migration
completed.summary: Migrated all canonical 4-point Sizes.p* presentation/widget candidates to CatchSpacing.s*. The scanner now reports zero canonical candidates; fine-grained p2/p6/p10/p14/p18 compatibility helpers remain watch-only.
